topic: Evaluate whether Fabric should pivot from MCP-first AGENTS.md/rules sync protocol to MCP-first knowledge sustainment protocol for AI delivery teams (5-layer storage x 5 types x 3-maturity-level methodology)

module_map.mcp_server: packages/server/src/index.ts (createFabricServer registers fab_plan_context + fab_get_rule_sections only; no fab_extract_knowledge, no fab_lint, no fab_get_rule)
module_map.mcp_tools: packages/server/src/tools/{plan-context.ts,rule-sections.ts} — 2 tools total; 1 MCP resource (bootstrap README) at AGENTS_MD_RESOURCE_URI
module_map.rules_storage: Two coexisting paths: (1) production = .fabric/rules/*.md indexed via .fabric/agents.meta.json (rule-meta-builder.ts:278 findFabricRuleFiles); (2) example/legacy = .fabric/agents/*.md (werewolf stub + agents-meta.ts uses .fabric/agents/ prefix). Internal toAgentsCompatiblePath() at rule-meta-builder.ts:559 bridges them.
module_map.rule_frontmatter: packages/server/src/services/rule-meta-builder.ts:709-792 — hand-rolled regex parser (NOT gray-matter); supports stable_id (<!-- fab:rule-id ... -->), summary, intent_clues, tech_stack, impact, must_read_if, entities. No 'type' field, no 'maturity' field, no 'topic' field, no 'last_validated_at' field.
module_map.rule_sections: packages/server/src/services/rule-sections.ts:12-17 — exactly 4 hard-coded section names: MISSION_STATEMENT, MANDATORY_INJECTION, BUSINESS_LOGIC_CHUNKS, CONTEXT_INFO. parseRuleSections at line 65 reads ## [SECTION_NAME] markdown headings.
module_map.agents_meta: packages/shared/src/schemas/agents-meta.ts — stable_id derived from path; layer derived from path depth (L0=root, L1=depth<=2, L2=deeper) at deriveAgentsMetaLayer:114; topology_type ∈ {mirror, cross-cutting, domain, local, global}.
module_map.events_ledger: .fabric/events.jsonl (canonical). Schema in packages/shared/src/schemas/event-ledger.ts — 17 discriminated event types: rule_context_planned, rule_selection, rule_sections_fetched, edit_intent_checked, rule_drift_detected, rule_baseline_accepted, baseline_synced (legacy), mcp_event, reapply_completed, event_ledger_truncated, mcp_config_migrated, meta_reconciled, meta_reconciled_on_startup, claude_skill_path_migrated, claude_hook_path_migrated, codex_skill_path_migrated, legacy_client_path_present.
module_map.doctor: packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts — 19 checks (line 285-305), separates fixable_error/manual_error/warning/info, has runDoctorReport + runDoctorFix; --fix repairs deterministic derived state only (agents.meta.json, rule-test.index.json, missing events.jsonl, deterministic bootstrap README, stale hashes).
module_map.init_flow: packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts — wizard + 3 stages (bootstrap, mcp, hooks); buildInitFabricPlan at line 521 writes .fabric/{bootstrap/README.md, INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md, agents.meta.json, events.jsonl, forensic.json} + .claude/.codex skills + hooks. INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md is generated by buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown at line 1390 from forensic report.
module_map.cli_commands: packages/cli/src/commands/index.ts — exactly 4 public commands: init, scan, serve, doctor. Aliased as `fab`. Internal commands (bootstrap, hooks, config) exist as submodules but not registered.
module_map.rule_test_index: packages/shared/src/schemas/rule-test-index.ts — V1 sidecar tracking rule<->test annotations (// @fabric-verify <stable_id>). Tracks orphan_annotations (test references rule that doesn't exist) but no rule-to-rule contradiction detection, no staleness-by-time detection.
module_map.dashboard: packages/dashboard/src/views/ — 4 views: health, readiness, rules-explain, timeline. Read-only consumer of /api/* endpoints.
module_map.fallback_legacy: packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/{GEMINI.md, roo-fabric.md, windsurf-fabric.md} still present (memory note: dropped Windsurf/Roo Code/Gemini CLI from supported set, but bootstrap templates remain in repo).

patterns: Three-tier rule progressive disclosure (L0/L1/L2) IS implemented end-to-end: deriveAgentsMetaLayer (agents-meta.ts:114) → planContext returns description_index with required+selectable booleans → rule-sections enforces L2>L1>L0 precedence (rule-sections.ts:40 PRIORITY_ORDER, api-contracts.ts:196 precedence tuple).
Selection token pattern: planContext returns selection_token (5-min TTL, in-memory Map at plan-context.ts:71); rule-sections requires it. Two-call workflow is enforced server-side.
stable_id has dual identity_source: 'declared' (via <!-- fab:rule-id name --> marker, regex at rule-meta-builder.ts:711) vs 'derived' (from path). plan-context emits warning for derived IDs.

Rule frontmatter uses lightweight YAML-like inline parser (NOT a real YAML parser): scalars + flow-style arrays only (extractInlineArray at rule-meta-builder.ts:774 splits on comma). Implications: adopting nested fields like `maturity: {level: validated, last_review: 2026-05-10}` would require parser upgrade.

Rule sections use markdown heading convention `## [SECTION_NAME]` parsed by regex at rule-sections.ts:88 — adding a new section type requires editing RULE_SECTION_NAMES enum + adding to ruleSectionsInputSchema (api-contracts.ts:173).
Event ledger is a discriminated union — adding new event types is additive (no migration needed for old readers if union grows). 'mcp_event' is a generic catch-all wrapper.

Doctor architecture is per-check function returning DoctorCheck with fixable/manual classification. Adding a 'lint mode' (orphan/contradiction/staleness) means: add inspect*() functions + create*Check() factory + push to checks array at doctor.ts:285. No structural refactor needed.

INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md is BUILT from forensic.json at init time, not seeded from a template (init.ts:548 buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown). Content is L0/L1/L2 structural taxonomy ONLY — does NOT contain model/decision/guideline/pitfall/process knowledge-type taxonomy.

rule-sync.ts has two entry points (line 32-69): ensureRulesFresh (hot-path, no rewrite) and reconcileRules (full scan, rewrites meta). Both emit ledger events but only reconcileRules calls writeRuleMeta.

Rules dir policy is 'flat' under .fabric/rules/ — depth derives layer (rule-meta-builder.ts:481-486 deriveNodeId), no nested taxonomy directories like .fabric/rules/decisions/, .fabric/rules/pitfalls/ etc.

relevant_files.annotation: MCP server registration. Exactly TWO tools registered: fab_plan_context (line 106) and fab_get_rule_sections (line 107). One resource: bootstrap README (line 109). REFUTES user's mention of fab_extract_knowledge — no such tool exists. CONFIRMS L0/L2 required + L1 AI-selectable architecture is operational. Refactor cost for new fab_extract_knowledge: register one new tool here + new service file + new schema; ~1-2 days of plumbing.
relevant_files.verifies_user_claim: Refutes 'fab_extract_knowledge already exists'. Confirms 2-call rule fetch workflow.

relevant_files.annotation: fab_plan_context tool definition (line 17-71). Description (line 21): 'build a neutral Fabric rule description index and selection token before fetching rule sections.' Pure rule-distribution language; nothing about knowledge ingestion or extraction. Refactor for knowledge pivot: rename to fab_plan_knowledge_context OR keep + add sibling tool — either way, semantics of 'plan -> select -> fetch' map well to 'plan -> select knowledge entries -> fetch matured knowledge'.

fab_get_rule_sections tool. Returns rules with hard-coded 4 section names. To support knowledge pivot's 5 types (model/decision/guideline/pitfall/process), would either: (a) extend RULE_SECTION_NAMES with 5 new section names — semantically wrong, mixes concerns; or (b) add new tool fab_get_knowledge_entries that uses a separate `type` field on rule frontmatter. Option (b) is cleaner.

MCP I/O contracts (Zod). Lines 53-114 plan-context, 175-216 rule-sections. RULE_SECTION_NAMES_TUPLE at line 173 is hard-coded. selection_policy at line 47-51 enumerates 'L0+L2 required, L1 AI-selectable' as a typed tuple — this is a structural commitment to current architecture that any pivot must preserve or formally migrate. CONFIRMS: protocol for 3-tier disclosure is fully typed and contract-locked.

Schema for agents.meta.json (line 74-77: revision + nodes record). Layers enum (line 12) is exactly L0/L1/L2 — extending to a 5-LAYER STORAGE model (article's L0 prompt / L1 docs / L2 wiki / L3 kb / L4 vector) would require enum widening + migration of every node. topology_type enum (line 13) has 5 values already. CRITICAL: stable_id derivation (line 93) is path-based — if pivot reorganizes .fabric/rules/ into knowledge taxonomy directories, all stable_ids regenerate, breaking ledger replay.

17 event types in discriminated union. ALL events are rule-distribution-centric (rule_context_planned, rule_selection, rule_sections_fetched, rule_drift_detected, rule_baseline_accepted, meta_reconciled, etc.). Knowledge pivot would need new event types: knowledge_extracted, knowledge_promoted (e.g. L1→L2), knowledge_marked_stale, knowledge_validated. All additive — no breaking change to existing readers. mcp_event (line 109) is a generic envelope that could carry knowledge events without schema changes.

Core build pipeline (line 57-77 buildRuleMeta). Frontmatter parser at line 709-792 is hand-rolled regex. To add 'maturity', 'topic', 'last_validated_at', 'sources' fields the article requires: extend extractDescriptionFromFrontmatter (line 748) — but the parser DOES NOT support nested objects, so maturity must be flat (e.g. `maturity_level: validated`, `maturity_validated_at: 2026-05-10`). toAgentsCompatiblePath at line 559 reveals legacy .fabric/agents/ namespace coexists with .fabric/rules/.

Section parsing + structured fetch (line 65 parseRuleSections). Hard-coded 4 sections at line 12-17. PRIORITY_ORDER at line 59 implements within-layer ordering. Diagnostic 'missing_section' at line 30 is the existing extensibility surface — adding a knowledge-type field to diagnostics is straightforward. No support for cross-rule contradiction detection.

Selection-token state machine (line 70-71 SELECTION_TOKEN_TTL_MS=5min, in-memory Map). buildRequirementProfile + path-based filtering at line 82-85. CONFIRMS: progressive disclosure (description_index → required + selectable → fetch) IS the existing protocol. The methodology's 'maturity-aware selection' would slot in at line 88-91 as another filter dimension.

19 doctor checks (lines 285-305). Already has: bootstrap_missing, taxonomy_missing (line 680), meta_manually_diverged, rules_dir_unindexed, stable_id_collision (line 277), legacy_client_paths. Existing rule-test sidecar handles 'orphan' (test refs nonexistent rule) at line 653. NOT YET SUPPORTED: rule-rule contradiction detection, time-based staleness, knowledge-graph orphan (rule referenced by no other rule). Adding these = ~5 new inspect+create check pairs; doctor.ts is already 1000+ lines of similar patterns — purely additive work.

Init flow (3 stages: bootstrap, mcp, hooks). buildInitFabricPlan at line 521 generates .fabric scaffold. INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md generation at line 548 + 1390 — content is purely L0/L1/L2 STRUCTURAL taxonomy (path-based bucketing of existing dirs/files). Does NOT contain knowledge-type taxonomy (model/decision/guideline/pitfall/process). REFUTES user's analysis claim that current taxonomy 'covers' the article's 5 types — current INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md has zero overlap with the 5-type knowledge taxonomy.
Confirms exactly 4 public CLI commands: init, scan, serve, doctor. No hidden internals. Adding `fab knowledge add/promote/lint` would require new command files + registry entries.

Single source of truth for fabric-init skill (per-client SKILL.md files derived from this). The skill has 3 phases: Framework Confirmation → Invariant Extraction (ban/require/protect categories) → Construction (write init-context.json + AGENTS.md tree + agents.meta.json). Output is L0/L1/L2 STRUCTURAL agents tree, NOT a knowledge corpus. The 'invariants' classification (ban/require/protect) is the closest existing analog to article's knowledge types but is project-constraint-shaped not knowledge-shaped.

Production L0 bootstrap surface delivered to users (8 lines). Hard requirement: 'before modifying any file, MUST call fab_plan_context([target]) then fab_get_rule_sections'. Locks user into rule-distribution mental model. Pivot story would rewrite this to 'before changing/learning, MUST call fab_plan_knowledge_context'. Tiny file, but it's the user-facing positioning anchor.

Sample agents.meta.json showing real shape: revision (sha256), nodes keyed by L0/, L1/_cross/, L1/<path>/. NO description, NO sections, NO stable_id (uses derived). NO knowledge-type tags. Demonstrates the existing 'rules per directory mirror' model — purely structural, no knowledge semantics.

Self-aware doc admitting (line 6-32) that this very repo's .fabric/rules/ is empty — only events.jsonl exists at root .fabric/. Confirms: Fabric the product is rule-distribution; Fabric the repo barely uses it on itself. RuleTestIndex V1 sidecar (line 53-70) tracks orphan/stale-by-hash but NOT stale-by-time, NOT contradiction.

Authoritative source-code map (lines 1-100). Confirms package boundaries: cli (init/scan/doctor/serve) → server (MCP+HTTP+REST+SSE) → dashboard (read-only) ← shared (schemas/i18n). Data flow diagrams (line 31-56) document fab_plan_context → fab_get_rule_sections is the only MCP write-coupled flow. Knowledge pivot would add a 3rd MCP flow (fab_extract → fab_promote_maturity).

Current public positioning (line 11): 'MCP-first, cross-client AGENTS.md protocol for six AI clients'. Lists supported clients = 6 BUT memory note + recent commits confirm only 3 are now supported (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI). README is stale relative to implementation. CRITICAL: line 25 says '.fabric/INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md keeps the first accepted domain map' — domain map, not knowledge map. Pivot would rewrite tagline + Architecture section + supported-clients list.

Roadmap stops at v1.2 (Portability & Trust). No v2.0 entry, no knowledge-pivot milestone. Themes are: v1.0 Control Plane MVP, v1.1 Observable Maintenance, v1.2 Portability & Trust. NONE mention knowledge sustainment, maturity progression, or AI delivery teams. Pivot is a green-field roadmap addition, not a continuation.

Repo-root thinking doc — describes a TWO-LAYER architecture (root AGENTS.md + docs/ tree, ≤300 lines, ≤4 nesting). PURELY about agent instruction distribution. Zero knowledge sustainment vocabulary. The hook+skill pattern documented at lines 104-251 (PostToolUse → flag file → Stop hook → block + force AGENTS.md update) is the user's MENTAL MODEL of Fabric — and it is rule-distribution, not knowledge sustainment. Useful evidence that the user's own design intent has been rule-distribution.

V1 contract test sidecar (40 lines). Tracks rule_stable_id ↔ test_file via // @fabric-verify annotations + hashes. orphan_annotations field at line 37 already implements ONE form of orphan detection (test → missing rule). Could be extended to rule → no test (the inverse), but cannot detect: rule-rule contradiction, knowledge claim vs runtime divergence, time-based staleness.

Two-mode rule sync orchestrator (line 32-69 RuleSyncOptions): incremental + full. ensureRulesFresh (hot path, no rewrite) vs reconcileRules (rewrites agents.meta.json). RuleSyncReport status enum: fresh | reconciled | errors. Pivoting to knowledge model: this layer is reusable as-is — change is ABOVE this layer (what counts as a 'rule' becoming 'knowledge entry'), not in sync mechanics.

Six client-bootstrap templates still in repo: CLAUDE.md, codex-AGENTS-header.md, cursor-fabric-bootstrap.mdc, GEMINI.md, roo-fabric.md, windsurf-fabric.md. Per memory note, only Claude/Cursor/Codex are supported now — GEMINI.md / roo-fabric.md / windsurf-fabric.md are dead templates. Cleanup is independent of pivot decision.

4 views: health, readiness, rules-explain, timeline. Read-only. To support knowledge pivot UX: rules-explain.tsx → could become knowledge-explain.tsx; timeline.tsx already shows event ledger (would surface knowledge_promoted events for free). No view supports authoring/editing — pivot to 'sustainment' would need a new authoring surface OR delegate it to the AI client itself (current design).

questions_for_user: Q1 — Naming/identity collision: stable_id derivation is path-based (agents-meta.ts:93). If knowledge pivot reorganizes .fabric/rules/ into directories like .fabric/rules/decisions/, .fabric/rules/pitfalls/, every rule's stable_id changes, breaking ledger replay (rule_context_planned events reference old IDs). Acceptable migration cost, or do we need a stable-id remap event in event-ledger?

Q2 — Section model collision: rule-sections.ts hard-codes 4 sections (MISSION_STATEMENT/MANDATORY_INJECTION/BUSINESS_LOGIC_CHUNKS/CONTEXT_INFO). The article's 5 knowledge types (model/decision/guideline/pitfall/process) are *types of rules*, not *sections within a rule*. Decision needed: are knowledge types a NEW dimension orthogonal to sections (frontmatter `type:` field), or do they REPLACE sections? Architecturally these are very different commits.

Q3 — INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md reuse claim FALSIFIED: I read init.ts:1390 buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown — it generates ONLY structural L0/L1/L2 taxonomy from forensic.json key_dirs + candidate_files. There is ZERO model/decision/guideline/pitfall/process classification. Your analysis claimed this was '1:1 reusable'. Do you want to (a) keep INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md for structural taxonomy + add separate KNOWLEDGE_TAXONOMY.md, or (b) rewrite INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md to mix both?

Q4 — Maturity model storage: article needs `maturity` (proposed/validated/deprecated?) + `last_validated_at` + `validation_evidence` per knowledge entry. Current frontmatter parser (rule-meta-builder.ts:748-785) only supports flat scalars + flow arrays. Are you OK promoting the parser to real YAML (gray-matter), or do we keep flat fields like `maturity_level: validated`, `maturity_validated_at: 2026-05-10`?

Q5 — fab_extract_knowledge feasibility from heterogeneous clients (Init Q6): Claude Code Skills + Codex Skills can be invoked, but Cursor has no equivalent skill mechanism — it relies on @-mentions of rule files. So fab_extract_knowledge would only auto-fire in Claude/Codex; Cursor users need explicit prompt. Is asymmetric coverage acceptable, or does the pivot's value-prop require all 3 clients?

Q6 — Doctor lint scope: current doctor (doctor.ts) has 19 checks but ZERO of them is contradiction detection (rule A says X, rule B says ¬X) or time-based staleness (rule's last_validated_at > 90 days). RuleTestIndex V1 only does hash-drift orphan detection. Are these net-new check categories acceptable as v2.0 scope, and are we OK that contradiction detection requires semantic comparison (LLM call) that doctor currently never makes?

Q7 — Roadmap conflict: docs/roadmap.md ends at v1.2 'Portability & Trust' with concrete release gates. No v2.0 / pivot entry. Pivot positioning is currently aspirational text in this exploration. Is the path: (a) ship v1.2 first then v2.0 pivot, or (b) skip v1.2 and jump to pivot-as-v2.0?

Q8 — Audience reachability (Init Q5): 'AI delivery teams' (B2B) is mentioned as new positioning. Repo evidence: only public surface is npm package + a single README. There's no enterprise install path, no auth/RBAC beyond bearer-token middleware (server/middleware/bearer-auth.ts). Pivot to delivery-team B2B implies feature gaps (multi-tenant, audit export, RBAC) NOT in any roadmap entry. Is this scope you want to commit to?

Q9 — Legacy bootstrap cleanup: 3 bootstrap templates (GEMINI.md, roo-fabric.md, windsurf-fabric.md) ship in templates/bootstrap/ for clients you no longer support (per MEMORY.md). Independent of pivot decision, should this exploration recommend their removal?

critical_falsifications: User's claim 'L0/L1/L2 protocol already done' = TRUE (api-contracts.ts:46-51, rule-sections.ts:40, plan-context.ts).
User's claim 'INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md already covers model/decision/guideline/pitfall/process' = FALSE (init.ts:1390 builds ONLY structural path-based taxonomy).
User's claim 'fab_extract_knowledge exists' = FALSE (only fab_plan_context + fab_get_rule_sections registered in server/src/index.ts:106-107).
User's claim 'doctor --lint can plug in' = ARCHITECTURALLY YES (doctor.ts pattern is purely additive), but no orphan/contradiction/staleness checks exist today; ~5 new check pairs needed.

User's claim '1:1 mapping between Fabric components and the article methodology' = OVERSTATED. Maps that hold: progressive disclosure (L0/L1/L2 ↔ tier-based load), event ledger (events.jsonl ↔ knowledge audit trail), stable_id (↔ knowledge entry ID), doctor framework (↔ lint pluggability). Maps that DON'T hold: 5-type knowledge taxonomy (zero overlap with section names + structural taxonomy), maturity field (no parser support, no doctor check, no event type), 5-layer storage (Fabric has 3 layers + 5 topology types — different axes).

refactor_cost_estimate.rename_only_pivot: 1-2 days: README.md + bootstrap CLAUDE.md + roadmap.md + skill SOURCE.md text edits.
refactor_cost_estimate.schema_extension_pivot: 1-2 weeks: add `type` + `maturity_level` + `last_validated_at` to rule frontmatter parser (rule-meta-builder.ts:748), extend agentsMetaNodeBaseSchema (agents-meta.ts:44), add new event types (event-ledger.ts), add new doctor checks (~5 inspect+check pairs), update INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md generator.
refactor_cost_estimate.new_mcp_tool_pivot: 2-3 weeks additional: fab_extract_knowledge tool (parallel to plan-context.ts) + new service file + new ledger event types + cross-client invocation strategy (Cursor lacks skills).
refactor_cost_estimate.full_v2_pivot: 6-10 weeks: all of above + dashboard authoring view + multi-tenant if B2B target + migration path for existing v1 .fabric/ projects + new initial taxonomy template seed for 5 types + reapply path.
